theme: What happens 72 hours before 200 people land in a foreign city

linkedin.text:
"Nothing went wrong" is the most expensive sentence in events. Here's the 72 hours that buy it.

Your guests see the gala dinner. They never see this:

T-72h — Full venue walkthrough. Every table, AV cable, emergency exit. A printed 140-item checklist.
T-48h — Room allocations confirmed, VIP preferences locked, allergen cards on every relevant plate.
T-36h — Airport ops briefed. Drivers, name boards, backup vehicles, live WhatsApp channel.
T-24h — Technical rehearsal. Audio, slides, lighting. We find 7 things to fix. We fix them.
T-12h — Team dinner. Every person reviews their role for the next 48h.
T-0 — Guests land. The event has already been run perfectly, in our heads, three times.

That seamlessness isn't luck. It's the 72 hours nobody sees.

📅 Book a free 30-minute call and we'll walk you through how we run yours.

#EventProduction #IncentiveTravel #CorporateEvents #EmployeeEngagement #B2BEvents #MICE #Leadership

What the 72 hours before your gala dinner actually look like 👀

T-72: Full venue walkthrough. 140-item checklist
T-48: Room allocations + allergen cross-check
T-36: Airport ops briefed. Backup vehicles confirmed
T-24: Technical rehearsal. Fix 7 things
T-12: Team dinner. Every role reviewed

This is what 'nothing goes wrong' really costs. It's not luck.

facebook.text:
When your team walks into a flawless gala dinner, they assume it just... happened. 👀

It didn't. Here's the part nobody sees:

Three days earlier, our coordinator is walking the venue with a 140-item checklist. Two days out, every room is allocated and every allergy is flagged on a plate. The day before, we run a full technical rehearsal and find seven things to fix — and we fix all seven.

By the time the first guest lands, we've already run the entire event perfectly in our heads, three times over.

"Nothing went wrong" is never luck.

What surprised you most about the work behind a big event? Tell us in the comments. 💬
